summary: Bug fix verified: Teachers can now see student answers when clicking 'View' button in AI Grading. The 'No answer' bug was caused by manual assignments not having question_id fields, which caused frontend lookup to fail. Fix applied in 3 places: backend adds fallback question_ids, frontend uses fallback lookup, and Gradebook now generates question_ids.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend fix (ai_grading.py lines 558-562): get_submission endpoint now adds fallback question_id (q1, q2, etc.) if missing - CORRECT
Frontend fix (AIGrading.js lines 475-479): Uses same fallback logic `q.question_id || q${idx + 1}` - CORRECT
Gradebook fix (line 243): addQuestion() now generates question_id: `q${nextNum}` for new questions - CORRECT
Matching answers display correctly with arrow notation (key → value) per lines 495-496 in AIGrading.js

test_credentials.test_assignment_id: assign_b3837ab1 (Capital Cities Quiz - created WITHOUT question_id fields)

seed_data_creation: Used pre-existing test data: sub_8f94f177 (Paul Figueroa, Capital Cities Quiz)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix verified. The answer display bug in AI Grading was caused by manual assignments not having question_id fields. The fix adds fallback question_ids in 3 places: (1) backend get_submission adds q1, q2 etc. if missing, (2) frontend uses same fallback lookup, (3) Gradebook addQuestion now generates IDs. Test submission sub_8f94f177 demonstrates the fix working.

backend_question_id_fallback.details: GET /api/ai-grading/submissions/sub_8f94f177 returns questions with question_id populated (q1, q2, q3)

frontend_answer_display.details: View modal shows 'San Juan', 'United States', 'Tallahassee' correctly

matching_answer_rendering.details: Matching answers display with arrow notation: Japan → Tokyo, Brazil → Tokyo, etc.

no_answer_bug.details: 'No answer' text no longer appears for questions that have answers

bug_rca.root_cause: Manual assignments created via Gradebook question builder did not have question_id fields. The frontend lookup `selectedSubmission.answers?.[q.question_id]` returned undefined because q.question_id was empty/missing, causing 'No answer' to display.
reproduction_steps: 1. Create manual assignment in Gradebook without question_ids (old behavior)
2. Student submits answers keyed by position (q1, q2, q3)
3. Teacher clicks View on AI Grading page
4. Frontend tries to lookup answers by question_id (which was missing)
fix_verification: Backend: get_submission endpoint adds fallback q1, q2, etc. if question_id missing
Frontend: Uses fallback `q.question_id || q${idx+1}` for lookup
Gradebook: addQuestion() now generates question_id for new questions
All 3 fixes ensure consistent ID-based answer lookup
